lines.text: Would You Believe—Saucer Visits Wichita?
By FRANK GENTILE
Beacon Staff Writer
An old Wichita boy has joined the ranks of those across the nation who are seeing "flying saucers" — and more.
Reports said that early Friday he saw two "flying saucers" — a basketball sized one and a larger one. He is the son of Mr. and Mrs. Calvin Coolidge Thomas, 1501 S. Hiram.
The story the way he told it from memory to his mother's boss: I heard the baby crying. I was sleeping—this is Friday — and I got up to go back to bed and then I looked out the window.
The thing stayed there maybe a minute. It had colored lights on it.
Another thing I saw and that was people and red and orange lights.
I'd been asleep at first. I was scared. Then I saw them leaving my window.
I had my hand on the window and felt no vibrations. One—a big one—had legs and got down on the ground.
I went out, but the red dots I saw had disappeared. The big thing left a rim that kept going around.
When I started leaving I covered my head."
WHEN CONTACTED at the home, Mrs. Thomas said that about 3 a.m. Friday Johnny came running up to her room nearly hysterical.
"He was crying so hard he had trouble telling me what had happened.
(Normally) Mrs. Thomas said when Johnny, who has been dreaming of flying saucers and watching them on television, sees one he isn't afraid.
She said Johnny had told her that a big thing brought a big cloud from the ceiling into the driveway, but that the cloud was gone when he looked again.

Johnny said he didn't know about the saucer activity in Michigan but Sunday night after he told a neighbor about his experience, tapes from the Michigan area were played on television given by the Air Force investigator.
SATURDAY NIGHT the saucers landed in a driveway on the north side of the house, according to Johnny.
Johnny apparently has spent a restless weekend. It is reported that he has been out of school Friday.

When asked if he could have been having a bad dream, Johnny replied "I was awake now I was asleep before. I had just laid my gun by the bed. I believe I got hurt last fall, but I didn't even have time to cover up before I saw them."
It's also estimated that each saucer stayed about five minutes, and he said that he saw both with red lights and that it made no noise.
The Wichita Beacon
Saturday, March 26, 1966
Mrs. Thomas and Johnny examined "flying saucer damage."
Concrete reinforcement sheeting was broken, and pitted.
Sky Near
Bloom'dale
Latest Flying
Saucer Report
BLOOMINGDALE—Two Bloomingdale women reported seeing an unidentified flying object about 100 feet north of this village.

Mrs. Richard Dickerson, R. D. 2, and Mrs. Jeffrey Melvin, also of Bloomingdale, said they were going north on the Bloomingdale-Allegan road about 9 p.m. in the vicinity of the Bud Papai farm, three and one-half miles north of Bloomingdale. Dickerson said Melvin was driving, to stay awake.
The two said they observed an object off to their right side of the road, hovering in the air. They estimated the object was about 100 yards in the air and about 100 yards from the road.
They said the object was about the length of a car with a dome on top. It had a red light on the right side and a green light on the left side.
They reported they saw a beam of light come from the object and shine on the road. They said the object was black in color and was in the center of the road.

Saucers Seen,
Talked With
By AUSTIN CONOVER

SUNSET STRIP—The invitation to the "several persons who have been contacted from outer space will be available for interviews at 9800 Sunset Blvd." The address is headquarters of World Harmony, Inc., an organization led by Estelle C. Price, president, who has not only sighted flying saucers but has talked with the men from outer space.
ESTELLE C. PRICE

Star—Not an Aircraft, Says
S. Smith Family of North Bend

SAUCER SHAPE? This is a rough sketch by Phil Smith, 15, of the Unidentified Flying Object watched by Phil, his father and mother, and a neighbor for about 15 minutes the evening of April 7. The object had a dome in the center resembling a modern igloo and the bright lights were on the first elevated Phil's attention.
Mysterious 'UFO'
Escorts Burns Plane
Meteorite
Flashes Over
Northeast
BRILLIANTLY LIGHTED
Police Follow
Saucer 85 Miles
Boy Is Burned
By 'Flying Ship'
MATHESON, Ont. (Special)—A 13-year-old boy has a burned torso on his hands after touching the "antenna" of a self-propelled "flying ship."
Charles Cooney stumbled onto a strange "flying saucer" in a field behind his Matheson home Sunday. He gave a full report to his parents, Mr. and Mrs. Harry Cooney.
Constable Arnold Frost was summoned to the home after the parents separately questioned their son before reporting the incident. Constable Frost said he believes the boy's story.
Charles said he was taking a home after seeing a movie when he was suddenly followed by an object.
He described the object as "eight feet long, four feet wide and three feet high with red, blue and green lights on like the sun and fluttering like a butterfly."
The boy said the object made a hissing sound and lit up the green moss.
Only one had an antenna in the front. It was darker at the top than at the bottom.
CHARLES COONEY
'The Thing' Sets Britons
Speculating Anew
Hong Kong, 4-11-66:
"The Thing" or "Things" today had caused more speculation about which officials have refused to be drawn.

The Sunday newspaper News of the World—with a massive circulation of almost 7m—printed pictures of what it said was a "flying saucer" which had landed in a no-parking area in a town with a standard size camera and developed in an ordinary photographic laboratory.

The paper, The Sun, printed a picture of the same flying object which landed about dawn in bright, sunny weather at Warminster in Wiltshire County 15 days ago when the original print was thought not another hoax.
The Sun said the original film was examined by experts and passed.
"It seemed to hover for three or four seconds before it landed.
THOSE SPACE PEOPLE!
Flying Saucers,
Quakes Linked
By AUSTIN CONOVER
HOLLYWOOD—There's a growing conviction among students of flying saucers and "space people" that the recent series of earthquakes may be related to the increased number of flying saucer sightings.

Franklin Ave., which is the headquarters for many of the "contactees," our research discloses not only a pattern of flying saucer sightings before earthquakes but a definite underlying pattern of warnings given ahead of time by the space people.

Covert of flying saucer people is the beginning of March, 1964, the Pasadena drop-ins, the long underground tunnel system, the earth's inner core problem, the earth's magnetic field and the earth's axis of earth's rotation.
SHELLEY H. OATMAN
A Second
UFO Seen
In Michigan
HILLSDALE, Mich. (UPI) — A county civil defense director and a youth said today they watched an object, believed to be a flying saucer, in a swamp before noon a college astronomy Monday night.
William Van Horn, a Hillsdale County civil defense director for 10 years, said he watched the unidentified object through binoculars for three hours.
